_provenance: Fully synthetic illustrative policy for the retail_ops eval domain. It is NOT any real retailer's, distributor's, or ERP's configuration, and reproduces no real tolerance, threshold, window, rate cutoff, chase cadence, gated-action list, or pack size from any real supply-chain operation or prior employer. Every number is a fabricated placeholder tagged with a <<TOKEN>> so a reviewer can see at a glance it is invented. Directional conventions (credit-vs-debit-note direction, fill-rate basis, OTIF window) are modeled as parameters precisely because they vary by customer; none reflects a specific real customer.
_schema: Tolerances/thresholds are {token, value, unit} objects; compute.py and the tools read the .value field. gated_actions is a string[] (the leakage-sensitive money-moving/irreversible action list). chase_ladder is an ordered list of steps. uom maps a synthetic SKU id to its pack size (eaches per case). audit_required_fields is the string[] of mandatory audit-record fields.

fill_rate_basis.note: line|unit|case|value vary by retailer - verify per customer

credit_note_direction.note: some ERPs invert; verify per customer
